content.verdict: Silica Hotel is the quieter, more affordable sibling of the Retreat — 35 rooms tucked a short walk from the main Blue Lagoon, each with floor-to-ceiling windows and a view over the lava plain. What makes it a strong honeymoon pick is the hotel's private guest-only bathing lagoon, a warm mineral pool reserved exclusively for Silica guests and far calmer than the public Blue Lagoon next door. The rooms are pared-back Scandinavian — pale oak, natural wool, big tubs, and generous daylight — and the on-site restaurant leans hard into Icelandic ingredients like Arctic char, langoustine, and skyr. Couples who want the Blue Lagoon experience with more intimacy than a day visit but without the Retreat's price tag almost always land here, and walk away feeling they got the better deal.
content.best_room: Comfort Room with Lagoon View — around 35 square meters, oversized soaking tub, picture window facing the moss-and-lava landscape, and a short heated walkway to the private guest lagoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and first soak
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Keflavík (15 minutes), check in, and ease jet lag with a late-afternoon soak in Silica's private guest lagoon followed by dinner at the hotel restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Blue Lagoon main experience
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend a slow morning at the public Blue Lagoon (included for guests) with silica masks and in-water drinks, return for lunch, and book a couples massage at the Blue Lagoon Spa in the afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Reykjavík and Grótta lighthouse
itinerary_7_nights.description: Day trip to the capital for Harpa, Laugavegur shopping, and a sunset walk to Grótta lighthouse, returning for a late soak under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Golden Circle
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day small-group Golden Circle tour — Þingvellir, Geysir, Gullfoss — with a stop at the Secret Lagoon and dinner back at Silica.

itinerary_7_nights.title: South Coast and Reynisfjara
itinerary_7_nights.description: Seljalandsfoss and Skógafoss waterfalls, the black-sand beach at Reynisfjara, and the glacier tongue at Sólheimajökull with a local guide.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lava caves and wine night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning visit to Raufarhólshellir lava tunnel, lunch in a greenhouse restaurant, and an Icelandic wine-and-skyr tasting at Silica in the evening.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final float and farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: A slow morning in the private lagoon, brunch, and a short transfer back to Keflavík for your flight home.

caveats: Silica is not adults-only and can occasionally host families in summer, though the atmosphere stays calm because the property is small and spa-oriented.
The main Blue Lagoon is public and very busy — go early (before 10am) or late (after 7pm) to avoid crowds, even though entry is included.
The location is remote — lovely for soaking and stargazing, but every sightseeing day requires a tour or rental car from the hotel.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — it is small (35 rooms), has a private guest-only lagoon, and sits in a dramatic lava landscape. It is a strong pick for couples wanting Blue Lagoon proximity without the Retreat's cost.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: October to March for northern lights, June to August for midnight sun and hikes. Early May and late September are calm shoulder-season windows with mild weather and fewer tour buses.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: A Lagoon View room, a Blue Lagoon Premium or Retreat Spa slot, and your Golden Circle and South Coast tours. Aurora tours can be booked on arrival based on forecasts.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Silica welcomes all ages, but the property is small and spa-focused, so the feel is generally quiet and couple-friendly.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Comfort Room with Lagoon View is the sweet spot. If you want extra space, the Premium Room adds a sitting area and a larger bathroom.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Keflavík International (KEF); the hotel is a 15-minute transfer away. Reykjavík is 45 minutes north.
